preamble: This phonetic transformation describes the historical sound change where a voiceless aspirated alveolar plosive (`tʰ`) undergoes lenition and velarization, resulting in a voiceless velar fricative (`x`). This involves a weakening of the articulation from a stop to a fricative, coupled with a retraction of the place of articulation from the alveolar ridge to the velum.
phoneticEffects: Lenition (plosive to fricative), Velarization (alveolar to velar)

examples.note: The historical sound change of *tʰ to [x] is attested in Xiaoling Junhua, a Mandarin dialect. This shift is notably blocked by the presence of a following front vowel [i].
Initial voiceless aspirated alveolar plosives (`tʰ-`) have undergone a shift to voiceless velar fricatives (`x-`) in certain Gàn dialects.

sources: Brill (2022). Encyclopedia of Chinese Language and Linguistics Volume 2 De–Med. Brill.
